_meta.why: Tool routing policy. Maps each available tool to its trigger conditions, skip conditions, latency cost, and RPM cost. Enforces minimum-tool principle.

triggers: query references specific repo, commit, issue, pull request
query asks about code in a specific repository
query asks about release versions of a specific project
skip_when: general programming question with no specific repo
conceptual question about a language or framework
user has not provided a repo context

triggers: query depends on prior session decisions
query asks about user preferences or project state
user references something said in a previous session
skip_when: session is turn 1 with no prior context expected
context window is within 10% of 65K ceiling

triggers: query asks for current version, price, or availability
skip_when: answer exists in model knowledge with high confidence
query is about established concepts not subject to change

triggers: relevant collection is loaded for this conversation
query asks about project-specific standards or APIs

skip_when: result is an estimate (round numbers acceptable)
